what_it_does: This file is a weighted, cleaned ledger of design and operational decisions produced during a wizard run. Each line pairs an integer weight with a terse decision statement, recording what was decided, rejected, deferred, or mandated.
observations: Lines are formatted as '<integer>\t<decision phrase>' with weights ranging from 9 down to 1.
Many entries are policy-style imperatives: 'agents must not...', 'prohibition of...', 'reject...', 'require...'.
Topics span agent methodology, UI/UX, architecture/layering, registries, vault semantics, verification, migration, and provider capabilities.
There are near-duplicate entries with different weights, e.g. 'full-auto mode selected' and 'uses full-auto mode'.
Several entries record explicit bans: on completeness/correctness judgments, external frameworks, tidy summaries, and human developers.
Some entries note unresolved or deferred items: 'workshop structure is not yet designed', 'open_questions', 'status set to awaiting-tim-direction'.
The file ends with a truncation note, so the full inventory is not present.
standouts: manual assist mode selected (weight 9) and full-auto mode selected (weight 6) coexist.
requires authentication / auth required appears repeatedly across weights.
agents must not judge completeness and explicitly forbidden evaluation of completeness or correctness.
core/ must never import from ai/, tasks/, ui/, persistence/, commands/, project/ and inward-only dependencies.
vaults remain unconnected raw material / vaults are raw/unified material.
sequential agent ordering is default proven methodology.
conventions: Tab-separated numeric weight followed by a lowercase, terse decision phrase.
Imperative, policy-oriented language without explanatory prose.
Uses rejection/deferral/selection verbs to record disposition.
Records both positive decisions and meta-methodological constraints.
concerns: The same decision idea appears under multiple phrasings and weights, which could complicate aggregation.
Coexistence of 'manual assist mode selected' and 'full-auto mode selected' at different weights may signal unresolved mode ambiguity.
No definition of what the integer weight means (frequency, confidence, priority, votes?).
File is truncated, so coverage claims cannot be verified from this file alone.
notes: A weighted, policy-heavy decision inventory from a wizard run, useful as a constraint and preference source but requiring reconciliation of duplicates and mode contradictions.
questions: What does the integer weight represent: occurrence count, priority, confidence, or something else?
How were decisions 'cleaned' or aggregated from raw wizard output?
Why are both manual-assist and full-auto modes recorded as selected?
What is the full set of decisions beyond the truncation point?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated by filename and content: it is a cleaned decision ledger. Actual content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: explicit bans on evaluation and external frameworks
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to capture a durable, queryable record of what was decided during a wizard session so future agents can treat these as constraints rather than re-deriving or re-litigating them.
novelty: It treats design decisions as a weighted, cleaned dataset with explicit methodological bans, rather than a prose design document.
contribution: It contributes a normalized, weighted inventory of decisions and constraints that a fused system can use to seed policy, avoid re-deciding settled matters, and identify points requiring reconciliation.
summary_for_embedding: A tab-separated weighted ledger of terse design and operational decisions from a wizard run, covering agent methodology, architecture, UI/UX, registries, vault semantics, and migration constraints.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve a cleaned, weighted record of wizard-run decisions as durable constraints and inputs for downstream design and agent coordination.
